title: Where Coordinates Dissolve to Current

features.name: coordinate-chip primary
features.desc-on-touch: A chip that holds latitude and longitude, but each time you read it, the numbers have flowed slightly downstream. The chip drowns in its own precision.

features.name: tidal-trace circuitry
features.desc-on-touch: The traces of the circuit board glow with tidal current. High tide lights up the northern path; low tide illuminates the south. The board navigates by moon-pull.

features.name: grid-dissolution pool
features.desc-on-touch: Where the circuit-board traces meet seawater, geometry becomes liquid. You see a map here that rewrites itself to match the tide, coordinates flowing like current.

features.name: signal-node secondary
features.desc-on-touch: A tower, smaller, that translates between grid-language and water-language. It has begun to dream in both, unable to choose.

features.name: confluence-recording
features.desc-on-touch: A moment captured when the grid was still readable, before current learned to edit. The recording plays in both directions at once.

features.name: tide-memory glyph
features.desc-on-touch: A marking that shows every tide this location has ever experienced, written in parallel lines that all flow the same direction yet all contradict each other.

inscription: the coordinates and the current became lovers; they read each other's movements; now neither one knows where it ends and the other begins

file.name: Tidal_Survey_Board_Archive_Synchronized.bin
file.note: A circuit-board recording of tide measurements, but the data flows like water—bytes streaming in the direction of the moon, coordinates that have learned to follow current instead of fixing position.

exits.label: back up to coordinates that still remember being abstract
deeper into the tidal channels where rivers become arithmetic
toward the open ocean where all grids dissolve completely
into the shallows where you can still see the board beneath the water
